game_info.title: Du bist der Einzige hier
game_info.concept: "Du bist der Einzige hier" is a first-person horror adventure game where players navigate a surreal dreamscape, a blend of memories and nightmares, using a virtual joystick on the left side of the screen for movement and swiping on the right side to control the camera. The core gameplay involves exploring the environment, interacting with objects by tapping them, and solving environmental puzzles to uncover clues related to a central mystery. Progression is non-linear, focused on discovering hidden pathways and specific interactions that lead to one of multiple distinct endings. The game's difficulty scales through the increasing psychological tension, jump scares, and the complexity of piecing together the narrative fragments required to unlock the true escape path.
game_info.description: Navigate through a haunting dreamscape filled with memories and nightmares. You are the only one here, or are you? Explore eerie environments, interact with mysterious objects, and solve environmental puzzles to uncover the truth. Find clues scattered throughout the dream world to piece together what happened and discover the path to escape. Your choices and discoveries will determine which of multiple endings you'll reach. The deeper you go, the more unsettling things become.
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move forward/backward and turn left/right
Space: Interact with nearby objects
Shift: Sprint (move faster)
Z: Toggle flashlight
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Tests basic movement and navigation mechanics. Validates that the player can move through the environment, turn in all directions, and reach different areas of the map without getting stuck. Tests collision detection with walls and obstacles.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Execute a series of movement commands to navigate through the starting area. Move forward, backward, turn left and right, and attempt to traverse different sections of the environment. Test sprinting functionality and ensure smooth navigation.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves through the environment without clipping through walls or getting stuck. Position updates correctly based on movement inputs. All navigation controls respond appropriately and collision detection works as expected.

TEST_2.test_description: Tests the optimal win strategy by collecting all required clues and reaching the true ending. Validates that the win condition is properly triggered when all objectives are met. Tests object interaction and puzzle-solving mechanics.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Navigate systematically through all rooms, interact with key objects to collect clues, solve environmental puzzles in the correct sequence, and reach the final escape point. Use flashlight when needed to reveal hidden elements. Execute the sequence that leads to the true ending.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: All clues are successfully collected through interactions. Puzzles are solved correctly. The true ending is triggered and GAME_OVER_WIN state is achieved. The win condition validates that the player has discovered the escape path.
TEST_2.description: Tests the optimal win strategy by collecting all required clues and reaching the true ending. Validates that the win condition is properly triggered when all objectives are met. Tests object interaction and puzzle-solving mechanics.

TEST_3.test_description: Tests interaction mechanics and inventory system. Validates that objects can be properly interacted with using the Space key. Tests that clues are collected and tracked correctly in the game state.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Navigate to various interactable objects throughout the map. Use Space key to interact when in proximity. Verify that clue collection updates the game state properly and provides appropriate feedback to the player.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Objects respond to interaction when player is nearby. Clues are added to inventory. Game state correctly tracks collected items. Visual and textual feedback confirms successful interactions.
TEST_3.description: Tests interaction mechanics and inventory system. Validates that objects can be properly interacted with using the Space key. Tests that clues are collected and tracked correctly in the game state.

TEST_4.test_description: Tests the flashlight mechanic and its effect on revealing hidden elements. Validates that the flashlight can be toggled and affects visibility in dark areas. Tests environmental lighting changes.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Toggle flashlight on and off using Z key while exploring dark areas. Attempt to discover hidden clues or pathways that are only visible with the flashlight. Navigate through poorly lit sections with and without the flashlight.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Flashlight toggles correctly between on and off states. Dark areas become more visible when flashlight is active. Hidden elements may be revealed only when flashlight is used. Visual feedback clearly shows flashlight state.

TEST_5.test_description: Tests the sprint mechanic and movement speed variations. Validates that holding Shift increases movement speed appropriately without breaking collision detection or causing navigation issues.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Hold Shift while moving in various directions. Compare movement speed with and without sprint. Test sprinting while turning and moving backward. Verify that sprinting doesn't allow clipping through walls.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Movement speed increases noticeably when Shift is held. Sprint works in all movement directions. Collision detection remains accurate during sprinting. No unintended movement behaviors occur.
TEST_5.description: Tests the sprint mechanic and movement speed variations. Validates that holding Shift increases movement speed appropriately without breaking collision detection or causing navigation issues.
TEST_5.strategy: Hold Shift while moving in various directions. Compare movement speed with and without sprint. Test sprinting while turning and moving backward. Verify that sprinting doesn't allow clipping through walls.
TEST_5.expected: Movement speed increases noticeably when Shift is held. Sprint works in all movement directions. Collision detection remains accurate during sprinting. No unintended movement behaviors occur.
